---
title: "Start Here"
---

## What This Section Does

This section helps you place yourself before you dive into the main topic tree.

Use it to answer:

- Where am I currently strong?
- Which prerequisites are shaky?
- How should I read this site?
- How should I study proof-heavy pages?

## Recommended Order

1. [How to Use This Site](how-to-use-this-site.qmd)
2. [Math Diagnostic](math-diagnostic.qmd)
3. [Notation and Symbols](notation-and-symbols.qmd)
4. [How to Write Proofs](how-to-write-proofs.qmd)
5. [How to Read Theory Papers](how-to-read-theory-papers.qmd)

## Rule Of Thumb

If you are unsure where to begin, start with the diagnostic and then choose one of these tracks:

- `proofs first` if theorem statements feel slippery
- `linear algebra first` if modern ML math feels blurry
- `probability first` if uncertainty and expectation arguments are the main blocker
